name: GCSEs
description: Core subject foundation taken at the end of compulsory secondary school.

name: A-Levels
description: Subject-specialist academic qualification, the most common university entry route.

name: BTEC
description: Applied, coursework-heavy qualification combining academic and vocational skills.

name: T Level
description: Technical qualification combining classroom study with an industry placement.

name: Apprenticeship
description: Paid, on-the-job training combined with structured study toward a recognised qualification.

name: Foundation Year
description: An extra preparatory year before a full degree, often for students without all required subjects.

name: Gap Year
description: A year of work, travel, or volunteering before continuing formal education.

name: Study Abroad
description: A degree, exchange year, or qualification completed overseas before returning to the UK job market or staying abroad.

name: Start a Business
description: Founding or co-founding a venture instead of, or alongside, a traditional employed route.

name: Undergraduate Degree
description: Three to four year bachelor degree, longer with a placement or integrated year.

name: Degree Apprenticeship
description: Earn a full degree while working and being paid by an employer, with no tuition debt.

name: Masters Degree
description: One to two year advanced specialist qualification, often required for research or senior technical tracks.

name: PhD / Doctorate
description: Three to five year original research qualification, typically required for academic or clinical specialist roles.

name: Professional Certification
description: Industry recognised credential such as ACCA, CFA, AWS, or CEng, layered on top of a degree or apprenticeship.

name: First Role / Graduate Job
description: Entry into the workforce in or adjacent to the target career.

name: Senior / Established Role
description: Progression into a senior, specialist, or leadership position within the field.
